summary: Design for lib.is_terminal_state(state) -> bool: a pure helper returning True for 'done', 'failed', 'blocked'; False for everything else including None and non-string. Two files changed: lib.py (one function after decide()) and tests/test_engine.py (8 parametrized cases in a new section).

ledger.what: The terminal state set is exactly {"done", "failed", "blocked"}
ledger.why: The issue mandates this set. decide() (lib.py ~line 963-991) defines 'done'/'failed' as the process-axis terminal values; PHASE_LABEL_DEFAULTS (~line 805-808) lists all three as stable end-labels; 'blocked' is what the engine writes for a gate node that cannot proceed. 'iterate' is in-flight, not terminal.
ledger.what_i_did: Verified in lib.py lines 805-808 (PHASE_LABEL_DEFAULTS) and 963-991 (decide() docstring and return paths). Confirmed against the issue specification.

blast_radius.why: A pure reader function — no state mutations, no file writes, no external calls.

reversibility.why: A one-function addition is trivially deleted or extended.

ledger.revisit_if: A new terminal state is introduced to the engine (e.g. 'abandoned'); the set would need expanding.

ledger.what: Unit tests go in tests/test_engine.py (new section at the end), not a new file
ledger.why: test_engine.py is the established home for protocol-agnostic lib.py surface tests (its own module docstring says so). The feature is a trivial addition to that surface; a separate file would add navigation overhead with no benefit.
ledger.what_i_did: Chose test_engine.py and a new '# Section: is_terminal_state' block matching the file's existing section-comment convention.

blast_radius.why: Adding tests to an existing file cannot break existing tests.

reversibility.why: Tests can be moved to a dedicated file at any time.

ledger.revisit_if: The team adopts a convention of one test file per lib.py helper function.

ledger.what: is_terminal_state is inserted in lib.py immediately after decide() (~line 991), before upsert_status_comment
ledger.why: decide() is where 'done' and 'failed' are canonically named for the process axis. Proximity makes the relationship obvious; 'blocked' is the third terminal state (gate nodes). Keeping related state-machine concepts adjacent aids readability.
ledger.what_i_did: Identified decide() at lib.py lines 963-991; the insert point is the blank gap between its return statement and upsert_status_comment at ~line 994.

blast_radius.why: A three-line insertion in a 1803-line module does not structurally change the file or any caller.

reversibility.why: The function can be moved or removed without consequence to any existing caller.

ledger.what: No CLI subcommand exposure is needed
ledger.why: The issue explicitly says 'pure function, stdlib only.' CLI exposure (python3 lib.py <subcommand>) is only needed when a GHA shell step must call into lib.py. No such caller is identified.
ledger.what_i_did: Confirmed the issue text specifies 'pure function' and found no existing shell step that calls anything resembling is_terminal_state.

blast_radius.why: Omitting a CLI entry is additive-neutral — nothing changes for existing callers.

reversibility.why: Adding a CLI entry later is a trivial _cli() addition.

ledger.revisit_if: A GHA step needs to branch on whether a state is terminal.
